verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअथोषस्युपवृत्तायां कुक्कुटान् कूजतोऽशपन् ।गृहीतकण्ठ्य: पतिभिर्माधव्यो विरहातुरा: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaathoṣasy upavṛttāyāṁkukkuṭān kūjato ’śapangṛhīta-kaṇṭhyaḥ patibhirmādhavyo virahāturāḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: As dawn approached, the wives of Lord Mādhava, each embraced around the neck by her husband, cursed the crowing roosters. The ladies were disturbed that now they would be separated from Him.

verses.sanskrit: वयांस्यरोरुवन्कृष्णं बोधयन्तीव वन्दिन: ।गायत्स्वलिष्वनिद्राणि मन्दारवनवायुभि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: vayāṁsy aroruvan kṛṣṇaṁbodhayantīva vandinaḥgāyatsv aliṣv anidrāṇimandāra-vana-vāyubhiḥ
verses.text: The bees’ buzzing, caused by the fragrant breeze from the pārijāta garden, roused the birds from sleep. And when the birds began to sing loudly, they woke Lord Kṛṣṇa like court poets reciting His glories.

verses.sanskrit: मुहूर्तं तं तु वैदर्भी नामृष्यदतिशोभनम् ।परिरम्भणविश्लेषात् प्रियबाह्वन्तरं गता ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: muhūrtaṁ taṁ tu vaidarbhīnāmṛṣyad ati-śobhanamparirambhaṇa-viśleṣātpriya-bāhv-antaraṁ gatā
verses.text: Lying in her beloved’s arms, Queen Vaidarbhī did not like this most auspicious hour, for it meant she would lose His embrace.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मे मुहूर्त उत्थाय वार्युपस्पृश्य माधव: । दध्यौ प्रसन्नकरण आत्मानं तमस: परम् ॥ ४ ॥ एकं स्वयंज्योतिरनन्यमव्ययंस्वसंस्थया नित्यनिरस्तकल्मषम् । ब्रह्माख्यमस्योद्भ‍वनाशहेतुभि:स्वशक्तिभिर्लक्षितभावनिर्वृतिम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhme muhūrta utthāyavāry upaspṛśya mādhavaḥdadhyau prasanna-karaṇaātmānaṁ tamasaḥ param
verses.text: Lord Mādhava would rise during the brāhma-muhūrta period and touch water. With a clear mind He would then meditate upon Himself, the single, self-luminous, unequaled and infallible Supreme Truth, known as Brahman, who by His very nature ever dispels all contamination, and who through His personal energies, which cause the creation and destruction of this universe, manifests His own pure and blissful existence.

verses.sanskrit: अथाप्लुतोऽम्भस्यमले यथाविधिक्रियाकलापं परिधाय वाससी ।चकार सन्ध्योपगमादि सत्तमोहुतानलो ब्रह्म जजाप वाग्यत: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāpluto ’mbhasy amale yathā-vidhikriyā-kalāpaṁ paridhāya vāsasīcakāra sandhyopagamādi sattamohutānalo brahma jajāpa vāg-yataḥ
verses.text: That most saintly of personalities would then bathe in sanctified water, dress Himself in lower and upper garments and perform the entire sequence of prescribed rituals, beginning with worship at dawn. After offering oblations into the sacred fire, Lord Kṛṣṇa would silently chant the Gāyatrī mantra.

verses.sanskrit: उपस्थायार्कमुद्यन्तं तर्पयित्वात्मन: कला: ।देवानृषीन् पितॄन्वृद्धान्विप्रानभ्यर्च्य चात्मवान् ॥ ७ ॥धेनूनां रुक्‍मश‍ृङ्गीनां साध्वीनां मौक्तिकस्रजाम् ।पयस्विनीनां गृष्टीनां सवत्सानां सुवाससाम् ॥ ८ ॥ददौ रूप्यखुराग्राणां क्षौमाजिनतिलै: सह ।अलङ्कृतेभ्यो विप्रेभ्यो बद्वं बद्वं दिने दिने ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: upasthāyārkam udyantaṁtarpayitvātmanaḥ kalāḥdevān ṛṣīn pitṝn vṛddhānviprān abhyarcya cātmavān
verses.text: Each day the Lord worshiped the rising sun and propitiated the demigods, sages and forefathers, who are all His expansions. The self-possessed Lord would then carefully worship His elders and the brāhmaṇas. To those well-attired brāhmaṇas He would offer herds of tame and peaceful cows with gold-plated horns and pearl necklaces. These cows were also dressed in fine cloth, and the fronts of their hooves were plated with silver. Providers of abundant milk, they had each given birth only once and were accompanied by their calves. Daily the Lord gave many groups of 13,084 cows to the learned brāhmaṇas, together with linen, deerskins and sesame seeds.

verses.sanskrit: गोविप्रदेवतावृद्धगुरून् भूतानि सर्वश: ।नमस्कृत्यात्मसम्भूतीर्मङ्गलानि समस्पृशत् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: go-vipra-devatā-vṛddha-gurūn bhūtāni sarvaśaḥnamaskṛtyātma-sambhūtīrmaṅgalāni samaspṛśat
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa would offer obeisances to the cows, brāhmaṇas and demigods, His elders and spiritual masters, and all living beings — all of whom are expansions of His supreme personality. Then He would touch auspicious things.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानं भूषयामास नरलोकविभूषणम् । वासोभिर्भूषणै: स्वीयैर्दिव्यस्रगनुलेपनै: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmānaṁ bhūṣayām āsanara-loka-vibhūṣaṇamvāsobhir bhūṣaṇaiḥ svīyairdivya-srag-anulepanaiḥ
verses.text: He would decorate His body, the very ornament of human society, with His own special clothes and jewelry and with divine flower garlands and ointments.

verses.sanskrit: अवेक्ष्याज्यं तथादर्शं गोवृषद्विजदेवता: ।कामांश्च सर्ववर्णानां पौरान्त:पुरचारिणाम् ।प्रदाप्य प्रकृती: कामै: प्रतोष्य प्रत्यनन्दत ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: avekṣyājyaṁ tathādarśaṁgo-vṛṣa-dvija-devatāḥkāmāṁś ca sarva-varṇānāṁpaurāntaḥ-pura-cāriṇāmpradāpya prakṛtīḥ kāmaiḥpratoṣya pratyanandata
verses.text: He would then look at ghee, a mirror, the cows and bulls, the brāhmaṇas and the demigods and see to it that the members of all the social classes living in the palace and throughout the city were satisfied with gifts. After this He would greet His ministers, gratifying them by fulfilling all their desires.

verses.sanskrit: संविभज्याग्रतो विप्रान् स्रक्‌ताम्बूलानुलेपनै: ।सुहृद: प्रकृतीर्दारानुपायुङ्क्त तत: स्वयम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁvibhajyāgrato viprānsrak-tāmbūlānulepanaiḥsuhṛdaḥ prakṛtīr dārānupāyuṅkta tataḥ svayam
verses.text: After first distributing flower garlands, pān and sandalwood paste to the brāhmaṇas, He would give these gifts to His friends, ministers and wives, and finally He would partake of them Himself.

verses.sanskrit: तावत् सूत उपानीय स्यन्दनं परमाद्भ‍ुतम् ।सुग्रीवाद्यैर्हयैर्युक्तं प्रणम्यावस्थितोऽग्रत: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvat sūta upānīyasyandanaṁ paramādbhutamsugrīvādyair hayair yuktaṁpraṇamyāvasthito ’grataḥ
verses.text: By then the Lord’s driver would have brought His supremely wonderful chariot, yoked with Sugrīva and His other horses. His charioteer would bow down to the Lord and then stand before Him.

verses.sanskrit: गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणी सारथेस्तमथारुहत् ।सात्यक्युद्धवसंयुक्त: पूर्वाद्रिमिव भास्कर: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇīsārathes tam athāruhatsātyaky-uddhava-saṁyuktaḥpūrvādrim iva bhāskaraḥ
verses.text: Holding on to His charioteer’s hands, Lord Kṛṣṇa would mount the chariot, together with Sātyaki and Uddhava, just like the sun rising over the easternmost mountain.

verses.sanskrit: ईक्षितोऽन्त:पुरस्‍त्रीणां सव्रीडप्रेमवीक्षितै: ।कृच्छ्राद् विसृष्टो निरगाज्जातहासो हरन् मन: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: īkṣito ’ntaḥ-pura-strīṇāṁsa-vrīḍa-prema-vīkṣitaiḥkṛcchrād visṛṣṭo niragājjāta-hāso haran manaḥ
verses.text: The palace women would look upon Lord Kṛṣṇa with shy, loving glances, and thus He would get free from them only with difficulty. He would then set off, His smiling face captivating their minds.

verses.sanskrit: सुधर्माख्यां सभां सर्वैर्वृष्णिभि: परिवारित: ।प्राविशद् यन्निविष्टानां न सन्त्यङ्ग षडूर्मय: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sudharmākhyāṁ sabhāṁ sarvairvṛṣṇibhiḥ parivāritaḥprāviśad yan-niviṣṭānāṁna santy aṅga ṣaḍ ūrmayaḥ
verses.text: The Lord, attended by all the Vṛṣṇis, would enter the Sudharmā assembly hall, which protects those who enter it from the six waves of material life, dear King.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपविष्ट: परमासने विभु-र्बभौ स्वभासा ककुभोऽवभासयन् ।वृतो नृसिंहैर्यदुभिर्यदूत्तमोयथोडुराजो दिवि तारकागणै: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropavistaḥ paramāsane vibhurbabhau sva-bhāsā kakubho ’vabhāsayanvṛto nṛ-siṁhair yadubhir yadūttamoyathoḍu-rājo divi tārakā-gaṇaiḥ
verses.text: As the almighty Supreme Lord would seat Himself upon His exalted throne there in the assembly hall, He shone with His unique effulgence, illuminating all the quarters of space. Surrounded by the Yadus, lions among men, that best of the Yadus appeared like the moon amidst many stars.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपमन्त्रिणो राजन् नानाहास्यरसैर्विभुम् ।उपतस्थुर्नटाचार्या नर्तक्यस्ताण्डवै: पृथक् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropamantriṇo rājannānā-hāsya-rasair vibhumupatasthur naṭācāryānartakyas tāṇḍavaiḥ pṛthak
verses.text: And there, O King, jesters would entertain the Lord by displaying various comic moods, expert entertainers would perform for Him, and female dancers would dance energetically.

verses.sanskrit: मृदङ्गवीणामुरजवेणुतालदरस्वनै: ।ननृतुर्जगुस्तुष्टुवुश्च सूतमागधवन्दिन: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: mṛdaṅga-vīṇā-muraja-veṇu-tāla-dara-svanaiḥnanṛtur jagus tuṣṭuvuś casūta-māgadha-vandinaḥ
verses.text: These performers would dance and sing to the sounds of mṛdaṅgas, vīṇās, murajas, flutes, cymbals and conchshells, while professional poets, chroniclers and panegyrists would recite the Lord’s glories.

verses.sanskrit: तत्राहुर्ब्राह्मणा: केचिदासीना ब्रह्मवादिन: ।पूर्वेषां पुण्ययशसां राज्ञां चाकथयन् कथा: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāhur brāhmaṇāḥ kecidāsīnā brahma-vādinaḥpūrveṣāṁ puṇya-yaśasāṁrājñāṁ cākathayan kathāḥ
verses.text: Some brāhmaṇas sitting in that assembly hall would fluently chant Vedic mantras, while others recounted stories of past kings of pious renown.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रैक: पुरुषो राजन्नागतोऽपूर्वदर्शन: ।विज्ञापितो भगवते प्रतीहारै: प्रवेशित: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatraikaḥ puruṣo rājannāgato ’pūrva-darśanaḥvijñāpito bhagavatepratīhāraiḥ praveśitaḥ
verses.text: Once a certain person arrived in the assembly, O King, who had never been seen there before. The doorkeepers announced him to the Lord and then escorted him inside.

verses.sanskrit: स नमस्कृत्य कृष्णाय परेशाय कृताञ्जलि: ।राज्ञामावेदयद् दु:खं जरासन्धनिरोधजम् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa namaskṛtya kṛṣṇāyapareśāya kṛtāñjaliḥrājñām āvedayad duḥkhaṁjarāsandha-nirodha-jam
verses.text: That person bowed down to Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and with joined palms he described to the Lord how a number of kings were suffering because Jarāsandha had imprisoned them.

verses.sanskrit: ये च दिग्विजये तस्य सन्नतिं न ययुर्नृपा: ।प्रसह्य रुद्धास्तेनासन्नयुते द्वे गिरिव्रजे ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ye ca dig-vijaye tasyasannatiṁ na yayur nṛpāḥprasahya ruddhās tenāsannayute dve girivraje
verses.text: Twenty thousand kings who had refused to submit absolutely to Jarāsandha during his world conquest had been forcibly imprisoned by him in the fortress named Girivraja.

verses.sanskrit: राजान ऊचु:कृष्ण कृष्णाप्रमेयात्मन् प्रपन्नभयभञ्जन ।वयं त्वां शरणं यामो भवभीता: पृथग्धिय: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājāna ūcuḥkṛṣṇa kṛṣṇāprameyātmanprapanna-bhaya-bhañjanavayaṁ tvāṁ śaraṇaṁ yāmobhava-bhītāḥ pṛthag-dhiyaḥ
verses.text: The kings said [as related through their messenger]: O Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa, O immeasurable Soul, destroyer of fear for those surrendered to You! Despite our separatist attitude, we have come to You for shelter out of fear of material existence.

verses.sanskrit: लोको विकर्मनिरत: कुशले प्रमत्त:कर्मण्ययं त्वदुदिते भवदर्चने स्वे ।यस्तावदस्य बलवानिह जीविताशांसद्यश्छिनत्त्यनिमिषाय नमोऽस्तु तस्मै ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: loko vikarma-nirataḥ kuśale pramattaḥkarmaṇy ayaṁ tvad-udite bhavad-arcane sveyas tāvad asya balavān iha jīvitāśāṁsadyaś chinatty animiṣāya namo ’stu tasmai
verses.text: People in this world are always engaged in sinful activities and are thus bewildered about their real duty, which is to worship You according to Your commandments. This activity would truly bring them good fortune. Let us offer our obeisances unto the all-powerful Lord, who appears as time and suddenly cuts down one’s stubborn hope for a long life in this world.

verses.sanskrit: लोके भवाञ्जगदिन: कलयावतीर्ण:सद्रक्षणाय खलनिग्रहणाय चान्य: ।कश्चित् त्वदीयमतियाति निदेशमीशकिं वा जन: स्वकृतमृच्छति तन्न विद्म: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: loke bhavāñ jagad-inaḥ kalayāvatīrṇaḥsad-rakṣaṇāya khala-nigrahaṇāya cānyaḥkaścit tvadīyam atiyāti nideśam īśakiṁ vā janaḥ sva-kṛtam ṛcchati tan na vidmaḥ
verses.text: You are the predominating Lord of the universe and have descended into this world with Your personal power to protect the saintly and suppress the wicked. We cannot understand, O Lord, how anyone can transgress Your law and still continue to enjoy the fruits of his work.

verses.sanskrit: स्वप्नायितं नृपसुखं परतन्त्रमीशशश्वद्भ‍येन मृतकेन धुरं वहाम: ।हित्वा तदात्मनि सुखं त्वदनीहलभ्यंक्लिश्यामहेऽतिकृपणास्तव माययेह ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: svapnāyitaṁ nṛpa-sukhaṁ para-tantram īśaśaśvad-bhayena mṛtakena dhuraṁ vahāmaḥhitvā tad ātmani sukhaṁ tvad-anīha-labhyaṁkliśyāmahe ’ti-kṛpaṇās tava māyayeha
verses.text: O Lord, with this corpselike body, always full of fear, we bear the burden of the relative happiness of kings, which is just like a dream. Thus we have rejected the real happiness of the soul, which comes by rendering selfless service to You. Being so very wretched, we simply suffer in this life under the spell of Your illusory energy.

verses.sanskrit: तन्नो भवान् प्रणतशोकहराङ्‍‍घ्रियुग्मोबद्धान् वियुङ्‍क्ष्व मगधाह्वयकर्मपाशात् ।यो भूभुजोऽयुतमतङ्गजवीर्यमेकोबिभ्रद् रुरोध भवने मृगराडिवावी: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tan no bhavān praṇata-śoka-harāṅghri-yugmobaddhān viyuṅkṣva magadhāhvaya-karma-pāśātyo bhū-bhujo ’yuta-mataṅgaja-vīryam ekobibhrad rurodha bhavane mṛga-rāḍ ivāvīḥ
verses.text: Therefore, since Your feet relieve the sorrow of those who surrender to them, please release us prisoners from the shackles of karma, manifest as the King of Magadha. Wielding alone the prowess of ten thousand maddened elephants, he has locked us up in his house just as a lion captures sheep.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै त्वया द्विनवकृत्व उदात्तचक्रभग्नो मृधे खलु भवन्तमनन्तवीर्यम् ।जित्वा नृलोकनिरतं सकृदूढदर्पोयुष्मत्प्रजा रुजति नोऽजित तद् विधेहि ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: yo vai tvayā dvi-nava-kṛtva udātta-cakrabhagno mṛdhe khalu bhavantam ananta-vīryamjitvā nṛ-loka-nirataṁ sakṛd ūḍha-darpoyuṣmat-prajā rujati no ’jita tad vidhehi
verses.text: O wielder of the disc! Your strength is unlimited, and thus seventeen times You crushed Jarāsandha in battle. But then, absorbed in human affairs, You allowed him to defeat You once. Now he is so filled with pride that he dares to torment us, Your subjects. O unconquerable one, please rectify this situation.

verses.sanskrit: दूत उवाचइति मागधसंरुद्धा भवद्दर्शनकाङ्‍‍क्षिण: ।प्रपन्ना: पादमूलं ते दीनानां शं विधीयताम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dūta uvācaiti māgadha-saṁruddhābhavad-darśana-kaṅkṣiṇaḥprapannāḥ pāda-mūlaṁ tedīnānāṁ śaṁ vidhīyatām
verses.text: The messenger continued: This is the message of the kings imprisoned by Jarāsandha, who all hanker for Your audience, having surrendered to Your feet. Please bestow good fortune on these poor souls.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचराजदूते ब्रुवत्येवं देवर्षि: परमद्युति: ।बिभ्रत्पिङ्गजटाभारं प्रादुरासीद् यथा रवि: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācarāja-dūte bruvaty evaṁdevarṣiḥ parama-dyutiḥbibhrat piṅga-jaṭā-bhāraṁprādurāsīd yathā raviḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: When the kings’ messenger had thus spoken, the sage of the demigods, Nārada, suddenly appeared. Bearing a mass of golden matted locks on his head, the supremely effulgent sage entered like the brilliant sun.

verses.sanskrit: तं द‍ृष्ट्वा भगवान् कृष्ण: सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वर: ।ववन्द उत्थित: शीर्ष्णा ससभ्य: सानुगो मुदा ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā bhagavān kṛṣṇaḥsarva-lokeśvareśvaraḥvavanda utthitaḥ śīrṣṇāsa-sabhyaḥ sānugo mudā
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa is the worshipable master of even planetary rulers like Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva, yet as soon as He saw that Nārada Muni had arrived, He joyfully stood up along with His ministers and secretaries to receive the great sage and offer His respectful obeisances by bowing His head.

verses.sanskrit: सभाजयित्वा विधिवत् कृतासनपरिग्रहम् ।बभाषे सुनृतैर्वाक्यै: श्रद्धया तर्पयन् मुनिम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhājayitvā vidhi-vatkṛtāsana-parigrahambabhāṣe sunṛtair vākyaiḥśraddhayā tarpayan munim
verses.text: After Nārada had accepted the seat offered to him, Lord Kṛṣṇa honored the sage according to scriptural injunctions and, gratifying him with His reverence, spoke the following truthful and pleasing words.

verses.sanskrit: अपि स्विदद्य लोकानां त्रयाणामकुतोभयम् ।ननु भूयान् भगवतो लोकान् पर्यटतो गुण: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: api svid adya lokānāṁtrayāṇām akuto-bhayamnanu bhūyān bhagavatolokān paryaṭato guṇaḥ
verses.text: [Lord Kṛṣṇa said:] It is certain that today the three worlds have attained freedom from all fear, for that is the influence of such a great personality as you, who travel at will throughout all the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: न हि तेऽविदितं किञ्चिल्ल‍ोकेष्वीश्वरकर्तृषु ।अथ पृच्छामहे युष्मान्पाण्डवानां चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hi te ’viditaṁ kiñcillokeṣv īśvara-kartṛṣuatha pṛcchāmahe yuṣmānpāṇḍavānāṁ cikīrṣitam
verses.text: There is nothing unknown to you within God’s creation. Therefore please tell Us what the Pāṇḍavas intend to do.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीनारद उवाचद‍ृष्टा मया ते बहुशो दुरत्ययामाया विभो विश्वसृजश्च मायिन: ।भूतेषु भूमंश्चरत: स्वशक्तिभि-र्वह्नेरिवच्छन्नरुचो न मेऽद्भ‍ुतम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-nārada uvācadṛṣṭā māyā te bahuśo duratyayāmāyā vibho viśva-sṛjaś ca māyinaḥbhūteṣu bhūmaṁś carataḥ sva-śaktibhirvahner iva cchanna-ruco na me ’dbhutam
verses.text: Śrī Nārada said: I have seen many times the insurmountable power of Your Māyā, O almighty one, by which You bewilder even the creator of the universe, Brahmā. O all-encompassing Lord, it does not surprise me that You disguise Yourself by Your own energies while moving among the created beings, as a fire covers its own light with smoke.

verses.sanskrit: तवेहितं कोऽर्हति साधु वेदितुंस्वमाययेदं सृजतो नियच्छत: ।यद् विद्यमानात्मतयावभासतेतस्मै नमस्ते स्वविलक्षणात्मने ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tavehitaṁ ko ’rhati sādhu vedituṁsva-māyayedaṁ sṛjato niyacchataḥyad vidyamānātmatayāvabhāsatetasmai namas te sva-vilakṣaṇātmane
verses.text: Who can properly understand Your purpose? With Your material energy You expand and also withdraw this creation, which thus appears to have substantial existence. Obeisances to You, whose transcendental position is inconceivable.

verses.sanskrit: जीवस्य य: संसरतो विमोक्षणंन जानतोऽनर्थवहाच्छरीरत: ।लीलावतारै: स्वयश:प्रदीपकंप्राज्वालयत्त्वा तमहं प्रपद्ये ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: jīvasya yaḥ saṁsarato vimokṣaṇaṁna jānato ’nartha-vahāc charīrataḥlīlāvatāraiḥ sva-yaśaḥ pradīpakaṁprājvālayat tvā tam ahaṁ prapadye
verses.text: The living being caught in the cycle of birth and death does not know how he can be delivered from the material body, which brings him so much trouble. But You, the Supreme Lord, descend to this world in various personal forms, and by performing Your pastimes You illumine the soul’s path with the blazing torch of Your fame. Therefore I surrender unto You.

verses.sanskrit: अथाप्याश्रावये ब्रह्म नरलोकविडम्बनम् ।राज्ञ: पैतृष्वस्रेयस्य भक्तस्य च चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: athāpy āśrāvaye brahmanara-loka-viḍambanamrājñaḥ paitṛ-ṣvasreyasyabhaktasya ca cikīrṣitam
verses.text: Nonetheless, O Supreme Truth playing the part of a human being, I shall tell You what Your devotee Yudhiṣṭhira Mahārāja, the son of Your father’s sister, intends to do.

verses.sanskrit: यक्ष्यति त्वां मखेन्द्रेण राजसूयेन पाण्डव: ।पारमेष्ठ्यकामो नृपतिस्तद् भवाननुमोदताम् ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yakṣyati tvāṁ makhendreṇarājasūyena pāṇḍavaḥpārameṣṭhya-kāmo nṛpatistad bhavān anumodatām
verses.text: Desiring unrivaled sovereignty, King Yudhiṣṭhira intends to worship You with the greatest fire sacrifice, the Rājasūya. Please bless his endeavor.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् देव क्रतुवरे भवन्तं वै सुरादय: ।दिद‍ृक्षव: समेष्यन्ति राजानश्च यशस्विन: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin deva kratu-varebhavantaṁ vai surādayaḥdidṛkṣavaḥ sameṣyantirājānaś ca yaśasvinaḥ
verses.text: O Lord, exalted demigods and glorious kings, eager to see You, will all come to that best of sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: श्रवणत्कीर्तनाद् ध्यानात्पूयन्तेऽन्तेवसायिन: ।तव ब्रह्ममयस्येश किमुतेक्षाभिमर्शिन: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śravaṇāt kīrtanād dhyānātpūyante ’nte-vasāyinaḥtava brahma-mayasyeśakim utekṣābhimarśinaḥ
verses.text: O Lord, even outcastes are purified by hearing and chanting Your glories and meditating upon You, the Absolute Truth. What then to speak of those who see and touch You?

verses.sanskrit: यस्यामलं दिवि यश: प्रथितं रसायांभूमौ च ते भुवनमङ्गल दिग्वितानम् ।मन्दाकिनीति दिवि भोगवतीति चाधोगङ्गेति चेह चरणाम्बु पुनाति विश्वम् ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasyāmalaṁ divi yaśaḥ prathitaṁ rasāyāṁbhūmau ca te bhuvana-maṅgala dig-vitānammandākinīti divi bhogavatīti cādhogaṅgeti ceha caraṇāmbu punāti viśvam
verses.text: My dear Lord, You are the symbol of everything auspicious. Your transcendental name and fame is spread like a canopy all over the universe, including the higher, middle and lower planetary systems. The transcendental water that washes Your lotus feet is known in the higher planetary systems as the Mandākinī River, in the lower planetary systems as the Bhogavatī and in this earthly planetary system as the Ganges. This sacred, transcendental water flows throughout the entire universe, purifying wherever it goes.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचतत्र तेष्वात्मपक्षेष्वगृणत्सु विजिगीषया ।वाच: पेशै: स्मयन् भृत्यमुद्धवं प्राह केशव: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatatra teṣv ātma-pakṣeṣv a-gṛṇatsu vijigīṣayāvācaḥ peśaiḥ smayan bhṛtyamuddhavaṁ prāha keśavaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: When His supporters, the Yādavas, objected to this proposal out of eagerness to defeat Jarāsandha, Lord Keśava turned to His servant Uddhava and, smiling, addressed him with fine words.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचत्वं हि न: परमं चक्षु: सुहृन्मन्त्रार्थतत्त्ववित् ।अथात्र ब्रूह्यनुष्ठेयं श्रद्दध्म: करवाम तत् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācatvaṁ hi naḥ paramaṁ cakṣuḥsuhṛn mantrārtha-tattva-vitathātra brūhy anuṣṭheyaṁśraddadhmaḥ karavāma tat
verses.text: The Personality of Godhead said: You are indeed Our best eye and closest friend, for you know perfectly the relative value of various kinds of counsel. Therefore please tell Us what should be done in this situation. We trust your judgment and shall do as you say.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युपामन्त्रितो भर्त्रा सर्वज्ञेनापि मुग्धवत् ।निदेशं शिरसाधाय उद्धव: प्रत्यभाषत ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity upāmantrito bhartrāsarva-jñenāpi mugdha-vatnideśaṁ śirasādhāyauddhavaḥ pratyabhāṣata
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thus requested by his master, who, though omniscient, acted as if perplexed, Uddhava took this order upon his head and replied as follows.
